analysis: Seven nights of data, and the story is honestly pretty interesting. Your best night had you down before 9:15pm with a solid chunk of deep sleep early in the cycle, which is exactly where you want it — your body was doing the thing. The rougher stretch was that early-hours fragmented run where you cycled through core, tiny slivers of deep, two REM bursts, and three separate wake windows between 1am and 5am, which tells me something was pulling you out of the deeper stages right when they mattered most. The wind-down stack you're running is genuinely good, so the gap isn't effort, it's probably consistency — getting to bed closer to that 8:30 target more nights, not just the ones where everything lines up. You're building something real here, even on the broken nights.
